description: Arguments the model emits for a tool are passed straight into execution without schema validation or permission gating.
body: Failure mode: a tool-calling loop takes the model's proposed JSON arguments and invokes the tool directly, so an out-of-range id, an unexpected path, or an injected value reaches a side effect. Detection signals: no validator between the model's arguments and the tool call; side-effecting tools lack a permission gate; no test feeds malformed arguments. Do not trust model-produced arguments or assume the schema constrains them at runtime. Safer fallback: validate every returned argument against the tool schema, reject or repair out-of-range values, and gate write/delete/external tools behind an explicit permission check with a negative test.
